taskCenter.title: Task Center
taskCenter.subtitle: Manage and monitor addon downloads and sync tasks
taskCenter.noTasksDesc: Active downloads or workshop crawls will appear here
taskCenter.dependencyPartial: Dependency check completed with failed nodes
taskCenter.syncDesc: Fetching and updating Steam Workshop metadata for addons
taskCenter.sdkDownloadWarningForced: This task is using Steamworks SDK downloads because the current settings force that path. Per the known limits documented in docs/steamworks-sdk-details.md, progress reporting, redownloads, cache cleanup, and Steam-managed duplicate copies can all be unreliable on L4D2.
taskCenter.sdkDownloadWarningFallback: This task fell back to Steamworks SDK downloads because the direct Web download path was unavailable. Per the known limits documented in docs/steamworks-sdk-details.md, progress reporting, redownloads, cache cleanup, and Steam-managed duplicate copies can all be unreliable on L4D2.

addonCard.author: Author: {{author}}
addonCard.addOrRemoveGroup: Add to or Remove from Group

batchActionBar.addToGroupDesc: Choose a group to add the selected addons to

groupHeader.desc: This group contains {{count}} addon files, allowing batch management.

groupModal.title: Create New Addon Group
groupModal.desc: Bundle multiple VPK files into the same group (e.g. Part 1, 2, 3 of a campaign map) for one-click batch operations.

linkConfirmModal.title: About to visit external website
desc: The link you clicked points to a third-party external webpage:
Please ensure you trust this website to prevent phishing and malware threats. Do you want to continue?

renameModal.title: Rename Addon File
renameModal.desc: Modify the underlying VPK file name. Using meaningful titles is recommended to prevent unrecognizable filenames with long numbers.

confirmModal.deleteGroupMsg: Are you sure you want to delete this group? This will not delete the files, only dissolve the group.
confirmModal.batchRenameMsg: Are you sure you want to auto rename the selected {{count}} addons? The system will name them based on workshop title and group info.
batchMoveMsg: Are you sure you want to move the selected {{count}} workshop addons to the manual folder? After moving, it is recommended to unsubscribe from them in Steam, otherwise Steam will redownload them on next startup.
...or go to Settings > Experimental > Workshop Detection Bypass
to move workshop items without unsubscribing.

moveWarningModal.title: Move Workshop Addon Warning
warningDesc: You are moving the addon from Workshop Directory to Manual Installation Directory (Addons).
After moving, please make sure to unsubscribe from this addon in Steam!
If you do not unsubscribe, Steam may redownload this addon every time you launch the game, resulting in identical files existing in both directories, causing resource conflicts or duplicate loading.
...or go to Settings > Experimental > Workshop Detection Bypass
to move workshop items without unsubscribing.

workshopWarningModal.title: Workshop Addon Moved
warningDesc: To prevent the game from re-downloading and overwriting, before executing {{actionName}}, the related workshop addon has been automatically moved to Manual Installation Directory (Addons).
You must go to the Steam Workshop to unsubscribe from this addon, otherwise the game will duplicate download the addon on next startup, causing conflicts!
workshopWarningModal.dontShowAgain: Do not show this warning again in the current session, and silently move

settings.languageDesc: Choose the display language for the application user interface.
settings.title: Game & Directory Configuration
settings.desc: Please configure your Left 4 Dead 2 game addons folder. The program will automatically access the addons folder and its workshop folder to parse and manage all VPK files.
settings.addonsPathHelp: Please select the `left4dead2/addons` directory. The program will scan the directory immediately after saving.
settings.experimentalDesc: The options here are in experimental phase. Enabling them may adjust the directory structure. Please use with caution.
settings.downloadDesc: Configure concurrency for the background download queue. Temporary files are written into the cache directory first and moved into the final workshop directory only after completion.
settings.downloadConcurrencyHelp: Number of add-ons to download simultaneously ({{min}} - {{max}}). Set to 1 for slow networks.
settings.maxDownloadRetriesHelp: Number of times to retry a failed download (due to network errors like chunk error or error decoding body).
settings.downloadTempDirHelp: Failed web-direct downloads keep resumable partial files here for retry. Manual cancellation cleans them up.
settings.sdkDesc: Manage Steamworks SDK query and download behavior. The Steam download path is currently recommended only as a fallback when direct URLs are unavailable.
settings.disableSteamworksSdkDesc: When enabled, both frontend and backend stop using Steamworks SDK workshop queries and fall back to web-based fetching. It takes effect for subsequent requests immediately after Save and Rescan, with no app restart required; however, Steam may not clear the “playing L4D2” state immediately, and in some cases you may need to fully close Left 4 Addons first.
settings.forceSteamworksSdkDownloadDesc: Disabled by default. When off, downloads prefer the Steam Web API `file_url` direct link and only fall back to Steam client installation when no direct URL exists or the direct request fails. When on, downloads prefer Steam client installation through the SDK. This path is fragile on L4D2: progress, redownload behavior, and cache cleanup are all unreliable, and Steam-managed duplicate copies may remain on disk.
settings.suppressSdkWarningDesc: When enabled, the frontend will not add a warning to the task center if Steamworks SDK is unavailable.
settings.sourcesDesc: Configure workshop metadata sources. Basic addon management always uses local files and local cache and does not depend on remote services.
settings.sourcePresetConservative: Conservative Auto: only search-related HTML while SDK is available
settings.sourcePresetSdkOnly: Steam/SDK Only: no Steam Web API or web crawling
settings.sourcePresetOffline: Offline Mode: local files and cache only
settings.dependencyRefreshDesc: SDK queries can usually refresh dependency relations safely; Steam Community page fetches remain protected by the global rate limit.

dependencyRefresh.cache-missing: Fetch only when missing from cache

settings.sourceHybridWarning: Using Steamworks SDK and steamcommunity crawling at the same time may make Steam Community IP rate limiting more likely. Enable this only when you need web-only author, tag, or page detail enrichment.
settings.sdkHtmlScopeTitle: Allowed HTML fetch scope while SDK is available

disabled.label: Disabled
disabled.desc: When the SDK is available, steamcommunity pages stay off except for single-item detail pages.

search.label: Workshop Search
search.desc: Default. Allow steamcommunity HTML for keyword searches and creator pages to compensate for poor SDK search quality.

navigation.label: Home / Navigation
navigation.desc: In addition to search, allow steamcommunity HTML for the workshop home page, browse views, and tag navigation.

all.label: Allow All
all.desc: Best workshop experience, but highest rate-limit risk. Even background silent refreshes may fetch steamcommunity HTML.

dummyBypassDesc: When enabled, moving an addon out of the workshop folder will automatically generate a dummy addon in the workshop directory (keeping only thumbnail, original AppID, version, prefixing name with (L4A Dummy) and marking generated by L4A) to attempt to bypass Steam Workshop synchronization checks.
Please note: Workshop updates might cause duplicate loads and conflicts between old and new versions.
settings.aboutDesc: A dedicated addons manager designed for Left 4 Dead 2. Powered by Tauri + React + Rust, it aims to provide players with a super fast and elegant VPK file management experience.
features: One-click Enable/Disable: Quickly rename `.vpk` files to enable/disable them in game.
Workshop Sync: Auto fetch and cache workshop cover photos, titles, and author details.
Group Management: Combine multi-part maps or associated addons for one-click batch operations.
Auto Identification: Built-in algorithm to identify campaigns/map packs and regroup them.
Physical Isolation: One-click transfer of workshop files to local addons folder to prevent subscription conflicts.
settings.licenseDesc: This project is open source under the MIT License.
settings.behaviorIgnoreWarning: Warning: Ignoring dependencies may cause addons to not work properly. Dependency checks will be skipped.

masterCollections.title: Master Collections
masterCollections.createDesc: Create a master collection to organize multiple groups. A group can belong to multiple master collections at the same time.

toasts.autoRefreshSuccess: External addon changes detected and refreshed automatically
toasts.groupEnabled: All addons in the group enabled
toasts.groupDisabled: All addons in the group disabled
toasts.autoClassifySuccess: Auto classification complete! Campaigns/map packs regrouped.
toasts.settingsSaveSuccess: Settings saved and directory rescanned successfully
toasts.batchMoveSuccess: Batch moved to manual addons directory successfully
toasts.batchRenameNoNeed: Selected addons are already in recommended filename format, no rename needed

home.mostRequiredDesc: Items most needed by other items to function
home.loadFailed: Unable to load Workshop homepage data.

browse.empty: No Workshop items found, try different keywords.

detail.title: Item Details
detail.author: Author
detail.importSuccess: Imported {{count}} items from collection as group "{{name}}".

deleteConfirmModal.title: Confirm Deletion
deleteConfirmModal.subtitle: You are about to delete {{count}} addons. Please read the following notices carefully:
deleteConfirmModal.workshopDesc: If the original Workshop item is updated after deletion, you will permanently lose the currently installed version. These files must be re-downloaded via the Workshop to be used again.
deleteConfirmModal.localDesc: The system cannot automatically download these addons via the Workshop. Even if they contain external links, deleting them may result in permanent loss, and they cannot be automatically restored!
deleteConfirmModal.confirmQuestion: Are you sure you want to proceed with the deletion?

dependencyPrompt.warningDesc: While a few addons may have optional dependencies, please ensure you have a good reason not to download any of them and understand the impact on the addons and the game.
dependencyPrompt.title: Missing Dependencies Found
dependencyPrompt.desc: These items are required dependencies for addons you have installed or are currently downloading.
dependencyPrompt.goToSettings: Go to settings to change default behavior
dependencyPrompt.cancelAll: I don't want to download any
